name: New Millennium Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving low-to-moderate income individuals and underserved communities across New Jersey, New York, and Georgia with personal and business banking products.
description_long:
New Millennium Bank received its New Jersey State Banking Charter on July 12, 1999, and began operations one week later. The bank has expanded to six physical locations: Fort Lee, New Brunswick, and Palisades Park in New Jersey; Bayside and Manhattan in New York; and Atlanta, Georgia. The bank positions itself as a community-focused financial institution dedicated to serving individuals, families, and small businesses in underserved markets.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking services. Personal banking includes free checking accounts and standard deposit products. Business offerings include New Millennium Business Checking, small business loans, business lines of credit, home mortgages, and SBA-backed loan programs designed for business owners who may not qualify for traditional bank financing. Their website emphasizes accessible financing solutions for startups and business expansion.

New Millennium Bank distinguishes itself through explicit commitment to community development and low-to-moderate income lending. The bank sponsors community development events, supports nonprofit organizations, and involves officers and board members in community institutions. This community-centric mission is central to their brand positioning, setting them apart from larger national banks. They market themselves as a "trust partner" with direct leadership involvement in community affairs.

While the website effectively communicates their mission and service areas, it lacks specific details on interest rates, account fees, loan terms, and minimum deposit requirements. The site does not clearly outline competitive advantages in pricing or features compared to other community banks. Prospective customers would need to call or visit branches for detailed product specifications and rate information.
services: Check cashing and standard banking services
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with six convenient branch locations across three states (NJ, NY, GA)
Free checking accounts available for personal banking customers
Specialized SBA loan programs for small business owners who may not qualify for traditional bank financing
Dedicated business loan specialists and personalized loan services
Community-focused mission with officers and board members actively involved in nonprofit and community development organizations
Explicit focus on serving low-to-moderate income individuals and underserved communities
Online banking platform available at nmbonline.com for remote account access
cons: Website does not display current interest rates, APRs, or specific loan terms for any products
No information on account fees, minimum deposit requirements, or fee schedules
Limited branch network compared to regional and national banks may reduce accessibility for some customers
Website lacks detailed comparison of their rates and terms versus competitor offerings
No online loan application process visible; customers must call or visit branches to apply

contact.address: 222 Bridge Plz S, Fort Lee, NJ 07024

best_for: Small business owners and entrepreneurs seeking SBA-backed or community bank financing who may not qualify for traditional bank loans
Low-to-moderate income individuals and families seeking personal banking with free checking and community-focused service
Underserved communities in New Jersey, New York, and Georgia looking for locally-invested banking relationships
diagnosis: New Millennium Bank is best suited for small business owners and low-to-moderate income consumers in its service areas (NJ, NY, GA) who value community-focused banking and may benefit from SBA lending programs. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal pricing transparency—prospective customers must contact the bank directly for rates, terms, and fees before making informed comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 222 Bridge Plz S, Fort Lee, NJ 07024
